title: inga cocleensis subsp. megantha
intro: Inga cocleensis subsp. megantha is a plant species within the genus Inga, belonging to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea or legume family. It is a type of tree native to Central and South America, and its medicinal properties have been utilized by indigenous cultures for centuries. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing pain and improving cardiovascular health. Inga cocleensis subsp. megantha is traditionally used to prepare herbal remedies, including teas and infusions, which can be made by steeping the plant's leaves in hot water.
